c.d: The range of operating systems and devices on which the app is available.
c.s: Official app websites, App Store listings

c.d: The app's ability to support multiple users working together in real-time.
The extent to which the app integrates with other tools and services (e.g., cloud storage, calendars).
The app's capability to function without an internet connection.
The presence of features like end-to-end encryption and two-factor authentication.
The ability to customize the app's interface, themes, and functionality.
The cost structure of the app, including free tiers, subscriptions, or one-time purchases.
The intuitiveness and aesthetic appeal of the app's interface.
The methods available for organizing notes, such as folders, tags, or notebooks.
The app's ability to handle various media types like images, audio, and video within notes.
